name: RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-27
title: Startup rabbit welcome message should display all active runtime status flags
description: The startup rabbit welcome message currently shows only a generic welcome, and the Stop hook emits no persistent status reminders. Two active runtime states are not surfaced anywhere in the session: (1) bypass-permissions mode (per-user bypassPermissions in settings.local.json) — not shown at startup nor at every Stop; (2) human-approval bypass (human-approval gate disabled) — missing from the startup banner. Users must know the active configuration at a glance on session start, and be reminded at every Stop hook print. The startup hook and Stop hook should each check all relevant status flags (bypass-permissions mode, human-approval state, any other configured overrides) and emit a visible warning/status line for each that is active — similar to the existing '[🐇 rabbit 🐇] 🔑 ━━━ HUMAN APPROVAL BYPASS ACTIVE — Step 4 skipped for all rabbit-feature-touch dispatches ━━━ 🔑' message that is currently only emitted in some paths.

history.old_value: The startup rabbit welcome message currently shows only a generic welcome. When active runtime states are set (e.g., human-approval bypass active, human-approval gate enabled), those states are not surfaced in the startup banner. Users must know the active configuration at a glance. The startup hook should check all relevant status flags (bypass-permissions mode, human-approval state, any other configured overrides) and emit a visible warning/status line for each that is active — similar to the existing '[🐇 rabbit 🐇] 🔑 ━━━ HUMAN APPROVAL BYPASS ACTIVE — Step 4 skipped for all rabbit-feature-touch dispatches ━━━ 🔑' message that is currently only emitted in some paths but missing from the startup banner.
